description_long: This object is a broad-leaved flowering plant with large, elongated green leaves radiating outward from a central base. Three upright flower spikes emerge from the center, each with a reddish-pink inflorescence and a slightly rough, textured surface. The leaves are thick, lanceolate, and have a subtle sheen, with some dark edging visible along their margins. The flower spikes are tall and slender, contrasting with the wide, low-lying leaves. The plant appears robust and healthy, suitable for decorative use in urban parks, sidewalks, or landscaped medians. Its overall form is symmetrical, with the flower spikes providing vertical emphasis above the horizontal spread of the leaves.
description: A broad-leaved plant with three upright reddish-pink flower spikes and large, green lanceolate leaves radiating from the base. The plant is symmetrical and visually striking, ideal for urban landscaping.
description_view_0: From this angle, the central and right flower spikes are most prominent, with the wide green leaves fanning out and some dark edging visible.
description_view_1: This view shows the central flower spike flanked by two others, with the leaves appearing more layered and overlapping at the base.
description_view_2: The tallest flower spike is centered, with the leaves spreading widely and the leftmost leaf extending further outward.
description_view_3: The central flower spike is most visible, with the leaves curving outward and the dark leaf edges more pronounced.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a whorled configuration around the flower spikes, with overlapping and layering visible, creating a dense inflorescence typical of spike-type flowers.
